changelog: All redundant exercises removed (Overhead Cable Ext D3 M1, Prone Y-T-W D3+D6 M1, Cable Woodchop consolidated, HLR D6 M1, Chest Supported Row repeated M2 D1+D4, Lateral Raise x3 per week M2, duplicate Band Pull-Apart warmups M3, duplicate Scap Push-Up M3)
Session order corrected: power always leads, conditioning always closes
D3 M1 restructured: arms removed, core + carries + shoulder health + Zone 2 only
D6 M1 upgraded: full arm block consolidated here from D3 + D2
D4 M1: Sissy Squat replaced with Leg Extension across all weeks
M2 D2: Med Ball Rotational Throws correctly placed before primary lift
M2 D4: Chest Supported Row replaced with Cable Row for variation
M2 D6: conditioning leads session, DB Lateral Raise removed
M2 (weeks 5-7): Flat DB Press added to D1 accessories — chest pattern maintained
M3 D5: Med Ball Slams + KB Swings correctly placed before Deficit Deadlift
M4 (weeks 13-15): full rewrite — single warmup, clean section order, shoulder health block, conditioning leads D6
GHR replaced with Nordic Ham Curl throughout all mesos
Deloads added: Week 8 (M2) and Week 12 (M3) — previously missing
Week 5 copy-paste error fixed — unique Dad Mission + Mindset per day
Shoulder health progression: M1 foundation → M2 adds serratus → M3 adds Trap 3 → M4 maintains peak stack

subtitle: A 16-week program for the man who refuses to let fatherhood be an excuse.
program_intro: This program asks for six days a week. That's a real commitment — roughly six hours of your week, taken from somewhere. Be honest about where it comes from. If it comes from screen time or early mornings before the house wakes up, good. If it starts coming from the hours your kids see you most, pull back to the four-day version. That version exists not as a lesser option but as the right option for full weeks. The goal of this program is not the program. The goal is the man you are when you're done with it — stronger, more capable, more present, harder to knock over in every sense. Some weeks that man shows up six days. Some weeks he shows up four. Both count.
